props.text: Revenue Analytics  ·  Customer Intelligence  ·  Market Position
Prepared by Lumina Analytics  ·  April 2026  ·  Confidential
Key performance indicators and high-level trends for the quarter

Revenue surpassed forecast by 14%, driven by enterprise expansion and new APAC partnerships. Client retention improved 2.1pp quarter-over-quarter, reflecting stronger onboarding flows launched in January.
Regional performance, segment breakdown, and growth trajectory
APAC expansion accelerated by Japan and South Korea enterprise deals. LATAM grew 75% QoQ following the São Paulo office launch in December.
Enterprise remains the largest segment at 52% of revenue. Self-serve, launched in Q3 2025, grew 340% to $2.2M and is on track to reach $4M ARR by year-end.
Acquisition funnel, cohort analysis, and satisfaction metrics
Funnel conversion improved 3.2pp from Q4 following the implementation of automated lead scoring. Top-of-funnel volume grew 28% through content marketing and strategic partnerships.
Competitive landscape, market share, and strategic outlook
Open Tokyo and Singapore offices. Target 60 enterprise accounts in APAC by end of Q3. Deploy regional data centers for sub-50ms latency.
Ship predictive revenue forecasting and anomaly detection. Natural language query interface for all dashboards. Targeting 40% reduction in time-to-insight.
Launch marketplace with 25+ certified integrations. Strategic partnerships with Salesforce, HubSpot, and Snowflake for embedded analytics.
Achieve SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certifications. Grow team from 180 to 260 with emphasis on engineering and customer success.
This document contains proprietary information. Distribution requires written consent.
